evidence_quotes.quote: Many modern video games require a constant online connection, even when playing alone.
If publishers reserve the right to shut down these services at any time, consumers are left without the product they paid for.

paragraphs.text: Dear European Commission, I would like to propose the introduction of stronger consumer protection measures in the field of video games. As a player, I find it concerning that I may purchase a game only to later lose access to its core features or the entire experience if the publisher decides to discontinue its services due to maintenance costs or strategic reasons. Many modern video games require a constant online connection, even when playing alone. If publishers reserve the right to shut down these services at any time, consumers are left without the product they paid for. This can feel unfairalmost like being robbed of something we rightfully own. I believe clearer guidelines or regulations could help ensure a fairer balance between consumer rights and business interests in this growing digital market. Thank you for your time and consideration. Best regards,
